title: How the System Works
subtitle: Technical reference for indicators, scoring methodology, and platform features.

scoring.title: Scoring System
scoring.p1: Every imported decision is evaluated with a score from 0 to 1, where 0 is the optimal decision and 1 is the maximum deviation from ideal. The score combines four weighted factors: hand equity, bet sizing accuracy, positional awareness, and MTT context (M-ratio and ICM pressure).

table.standard_meaning: Solid line. Minimal deviation from optimal.
table.marginal_meaning: Defensible action, but a slightly better alternative exists.
table.small_meaning: Minor strategic loss, relevant in the long run.
table.clear_meaning: Clear mistake with significant EV impact.

scoring.p2: The system considers MTT context when reclassifying decisions: under high ICM pressure (M ≤ 6 or final table), error margins are tighter — a marginal decision may be reclassified as small_mistake if the context amplifies the strategic cost.
scoring.p3: The Standard% of a tournament is the percentage of decisions labeled standard out of the total analyzed. It is the primary indicator of technical quality in a session.

indicators.title: Indicators
indicators.std_measures: % of decisions classified as standard in the period
indicators.std_interpret: Target: above 70%. Elite players stay above 92%.
indicators.avg_measures: Average raw score 0–1 across all decisions
indicators.avg_interpret: Lower is better. < 0.15 is excellent; > 0.30 indicates a high error volume.
indicators.clear_interpret: Directly linked to EV lost per session. Target: < 5%.
indicators.leakroi_measures: Estimated EV lost per leak spot in the period (in BBs)
indicators.leakroi_interpret: Prioritize leaks with the highest negative ROI — those cost the most.
indicators.icm_measures: ICM pressure level at decision time (low / medium / high)
indicators.icm_interpret: Incorrect decisions under high ICM cost more in the long run.
indicators.drift_measures: Quality decline trend across consecutive sessions
indicators.drift_interpret: Indicates tilt or fatigue. Reviewing recent sessions is recommended.
indicators.p1: Leak ROI is calculated by multiplying the frequency of each error spot by the average EV cost of an incorrect decision in that spot. High-frequency spots with low individual cost can outweigh rare spots with high cost — ROI captures that.

mstacks.title: M-Ratio Phases
mstacks.p1: Harrington's M measures how many full blind orbits (SB + BB + antes) your stack can sustain without playing a hand. It governs MTT strategy: the lower the M, the narrower the range of viable actions.
mstacks.deep_strategy: Full post-flop poker. Room for bluffs, slowplays, and stack maneuvers.
mstacks.mid_strategy: Pre-flop playability starts narrowing. Careful with big-stack confrontations.
mstacks.short_strategy: Focus on reshove/push-fold spots. Resteals important to maintain M.
mstacks.pushfold_strategy: Binary strategy: fold or all-in. ICM pressure automatically high.
mstacks.p2: Push/Fold decisions are evaluated with different criteria than other phases. The system applies push/fold tables based on minimum equity and position to calculate the correct score in this context.
mstacks.p3: Tournament stage (early, middle, late, final_table, heads_up) is detected by the number of active players at the table and complements M in defining the context.

dna.title: Decision DNA
dna.p1: Decision DNA is the player's strategic fingerprint — a 5-axis radar calculated from the complete decision history. It reveals structural game patterns independent of financial results.
dna.aggression_calc: % of aggressive actions (bet/raise) out of total post-flop
dna.fold_calc: % of decisions that resulted in a fold
dna.threebet_calc: % of pre-flop situations where a 3-bet occurred vs opportunities
dna.threebet_ref: Varies by position; > 12% in EP may be exploitable
dna.positional_calc: Aggression difference between late position and early position
dna.discipline_calc: % of decisions classified as standard (= Standard%)
dna.p2: The combination of axes generates an archetype: Nit, Passive Calling Station, LAG, TAG, Disciplined TAGfish, Aggressive Reg, or Balanced Reg. The archetype is a diagnostic starting point — not a definitive classification.

ghost.title: Ghost Table / Drills
ghost.p1: The Ghost Table converts your own leaks into review drills. Each "spot" is a recurring pattern where you made below-optimal decisions — the system extracts these spots automatically from your imported history.
ghost.p2: How the adaptive SRS works: after each drill session, the interval until the next review is adjusted based on performance:
ghost.mastery_next: Spot archived — maximum interval of 60 days
ghost.p3: The GhostDrillCard on the dashboard shows spots with overdue review (red), about to expire (yellow), and on track (green). The goal is to keep all spots green — a sign that the patterns have been internalized.
ghost.p4: XP earned: 25 XP per completed drill, 100 XP for reaching mastery on a spot.

compare.title: Tournament Comparison
compare.p1: Select 2 to 4 tournaments on the Tournaments page and click "Compare" to see a side-by-side analysis. The comparison shows:
compare.bullet_standard: Standard% and Avg Score for each tournament
compare.bullet_leaks: Top 3 active leaks per session with frequency and EV loss
compare.bullet_narrative: AI-generated narrative highlighting technical evolution or regression
compare.p2: The Delta (▲/▼) shows the change in an indicator between the reference tournament (oldest selected) and the others. Green means improvement, red indicates regression in the same spot.

coaching.title: Coaching
coaching.p1: When a coach is linked to your account, the system automatically creates a baseline — a snapshot of your indicators at the time of linking. All tournaments imported after that are compared against this baseline to measure real progress.
coaching.baseline_meaning: Average Standard%, Avg Score, and top leaks at the coaching start date
coaching.delta_meaning: Indicator changes since baseline — the primary progress metric
coaching.reviewed_meaning: The coach annotated or commented on at least one decision in the session
coaching.override_meaning: The coach replaced the AI-generated study plan with a custom one
coaching.effectiveness_meaning: Median Standard% improvement for students in the first 60 days of coaching
coaching.p2: Coach effectiveness is calculated by comparing each student's average Standard% in the 30 days before linking with the 30 days after the baseline. Coaches with ≥ 3 students with a baseline receive a verified public badge in the directory.

gamification.title: Gamification
gamification.p1: The XP system rewards study activity, not financial results. XP is persisted on the server and synced across devices.
gamification.event_drill: Complete a drill in the Ghost Table
gamification.levels_title: Level is calculated from the average Standard% of recent tournaments (minimum 5):
gamification.streak_desc: The number of consecutive days with registered activity (any XP-generating event). Resets if a day passes without activity.
gamification.ach_first: First Analysis — import and analyze the first tournament
gamification.ach_drill: First Drill — complete the first drill in the Ghost Table
gamification.ach_streak: Week of Focus — 7 consecutive days of streak
